message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Background</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>COVID-19 primarily affects the lung, thus the chest x-ray (CXR) is the first line imaging modality of evaluating the disease. CXR scoring systems for quantifying the severity and progression of lung abnormalities in COVID-19 pneumonia have been introduced. However, a chest X-ray severity scoring system has never been implemented in Tanzania to assist in predicting COVID-19 patient outcomes. Therefore, this study was designed to determine whether the CXR Brixia Score can predict outcomes among patients with COVID-19 pneumonia.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Materials and Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>A retrospective cross-sectional hospital-based study conducted on COVID-19 pneumonia patients confirmed by nasopharyngeal swab RT-PCR assay at Bugando Medical Center (BMC) using data retrieved from the hospital database. Data retrieved included socio-demographics and clinical information. Baseline CXRs were reviewed and assigned a Brixia score by two experienced Radiologists. Disease severity, length of the hospital stay and in-hospital mortality were recorded as outcomes and correlated with the Brixia scores by logistic regression analysis after adjusting for potential cofounders. A p-value of <0.05 was significant.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>A total of 220 patients were enrolled with a mean age of 59 (±12.7) years. A severe Brixia score had a significant likelihood of severe form of disease (aOR=4.8, 95%CI=2.35 - 13.67, p=0.001) and death (aOR=5.39, 95%CI=1.65 - 24.73, p=0.041). A severe Brixia score did not predict a longer hospital stay (cOR=2.29, 95%CI=0.49 - 9.72, p=0.293).</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusion</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>CXR Brixia Score can predict disease severity and in-hospital mortality particularly in resource-limited settings. It may assist in timely identifying patients in need of aggressive management, and thereby avert adverse outcomes including mortality.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Can the Chest X-ray Brixia Score predict COVID-19 disease severity and mortality in resource-limited settings? A retrospective cross-sectional study at a tertiary hospital in Northwestern, Tanzania

reference.unstructured: World Health Organization (WHO) COVID-19 dashboard. Available at: https://data.who.int/dashboards/covid19/.

reference.article-title: Association of Chest Radiographic Brixia Score and in-hospital mortality among Filipino patients with confirmed COVID-19 at a single medical center
Brixia score for predicting mortality and length of stay in COVID-19 confirmed patients at the hospital in Bandung
